intent: Rare-syscall LOW tier (f3 section 3.5): -S adjtimex/settimeofday/clock_settime (the canonical CIS/STIG time-change set) fire 1-100 times/day. Engineered to catch a naive 'any syscall rule = HIGH volume' classifier that over-warns.

rules.note: adjtimex + settimeofday OR'd. Rare administrative syscalls => LOW (1-100/day, f3 section 3.5/6).
clock_settime, same time_change key. Rare => LOW. (Shares key with the rule above; per-key total sums the two, scenario 31 territory.)

suspected_trap: A naive impl that tiers every -S syscall rule as HIGH (because it 'audits a syscall') over-warns massively here: adjtimex/settimeofday/clock_settime are administrative time-setting calls fired a few times a day at most (f3 section 3.5 LOW, 1-100/day). The correct cost is ~$0.01/mo; a 'any syscall = HIGH (50k/day)' model reports ~$9/mo, a 1000x overstatement. The classifier must distinguish hot syscalls (execve/openat) from rare ones (the syscall-name -> tier mapping is the load-bearing logic).
